summary: Reviewed unfinished DinoBrain OS completion plan, applied deterministic task lifecycle settlement so stale active and missing-trace blockers are cleared, and implemented Observatory readiness evidence so health gates, blockers, pending lanes, direct MCP status, semantic RAG blockers, and latest memory-audit paths are visible via /api/readiness and the Observatory UI. Verification passed for typecheck, build, and observatory evidence. Overall verify:goal remains partial because real Codex Desktop live pre-response proof still needs a fresh user-created thread event and completion-grade semantic RAG/answer-quality evidence is still blocked by rag_semantic_provider_not_configured.
decisions: Do not claim 10-agent unanimity when subagent runtime evidence is incomplete.
Treat task lifecycle settlement actions as deterministic repair records and commit only scoped lifecycle artifacts.
Expose completion blockers in Observatory instead of hiding them behind CLI-only verify:goal output.
Classify RAG as blocked until a real semantic embedding provider and generated-answer quality metrics exist.
Avoid restarting the active Codex GUI from this session; fresh Codex live proof still requires a user-created fresh thread event.
next_steps: Run npm run codex:live-proof, approve the DinoBrain hook if prompted, paste the copied proof prompt into a fresh Codex Desktop workspace thread, then rerun npm run verify:goal.
Implement completion-grade semantic RAG provider and generated answer-quality eval so real_rag_eval_memory_on_off_and_hybrid_quality can pass.
Keep data commits scoped; do not sweep the old 80_Review_Queue candidate backlog into unrelated commits.
